title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 75.
content:
There was an Old Man of Leghorn,
    The smallest as ever was born;
    But quickly snapt up he,
    Was once by a puppy,
    Who devoured that Old Man of Leghorn.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 75.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
